definitions.text: Traditionally the third of the four seasons, when deciduous trees lose their leaves, and temperatures and daylight hours decrease; typically regarded as spanning the months of September, October, and November in the Northern Hemisphere, and the months of March, April and May in the Southern Hemisphere.
The time period when someone or something is past its prime.
To spend the autumn (in a particular place).
To (cause to) undergo the changes associated with autumn, such as leaves changing color and falling from trees.
